summary: Mapped Apache FireFlow route ownership, aff-boot and dependency ordering, nearby PostgreSQL and ActiveMQ evidence, useful log families, and the bounded conditions under which ActiveMQ should stay secondary or be promoted.
follow_up_recommendations: Use the first-pass order Apache route config -> httpd state -> postgresql state/logs -> aff-boot state before checking ActiveMQ.
If core UI is reachable but background workflows or config propagation fail, inspect ms-configuration and ms-batch-application logs for ActiveMQ or PostgreSQL schema symptoms.
If cloudflow-specific paths are implicated, check ms-cloudflow-broker host-based routing and why ms-cloudflow-broker.service is inactive.
